title: Constants, Literals, Domain Types, and Configuration
description: Replace ambiguous magic values without creating meaningless constant noise. Learners cumulatively produce a domain constant and configuration policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish harmless local literals from repeated domain rules, units, limits, and protocol values.
Name stable domain values by meaning and keep them near their owning policy.
Use enums, unions, literal types, branded values, or value objects for closed domains.
